annotations.label: Repeating decimal

anchor.snippet: A repeating decimal or recurring decimal is a decimal representation of a number whose digits are eventually periodic

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Real.ofDigits`/`Real.digits` in `Mathlib/Analysis/Real/OfDigits.lean` for positional representations but no definition of an eventually-periodic decimal expansion.

annotations.label: Rational iff repeating or terminating

anchor.snippet: It can be shown that a number is rational if and only if its decimal representation is repeating or terminating

annotations.note: No characterization of rationals via eventual periodicity of decimal digits appears in Mathlib's `Real.digits` development.

anchor.snippet: becomes periodic just after the decimal point

annotations.label: Repetend / reptend

anchor.snippet: The finite digit sequence that is repeated infinitely is called the repetend or reptend

annotations.note: Grepping for `reptend`/`repetend` returns no hits in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Terminating decimal as decimal fraction

anchor.snippet: Every terminating decimal representation can be written as a decimal fraction

annotations.note: No notion of terminating decimal representation or its rewriting as a fraction over a power of 10 is formalized.

annotations.label: Alternative repeating-9 representation

anchor.snippet: every number with a terminating decimal representation also trivially has a second, alternative representation as a repeating decimal whose repetend is the digit

annotations.note: `Real.ofDigits_const_last_eq_one` formalizes the prototype `0.(b-1)... = 1` but not the general dual representation of arbitrary terminating decimals.

annotations.label: Irrational number

anchor.snippet: Any number that cannot be expressed as a ratio of two integers is said to be irrational

annotations.note: `Irrational x` is defined as `x ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℚ → ℝ)` with companion `irrational_iff_ne_rational`.

annotations.label: Terminating sequence iff prime factors of denominator are factors of base

anchor.snippet: A rational number has a terminating sequence if all the prime factors of the denominator of the fully reduced fractional form are also factors of the base

annotations.label: Order homomorphism from strings to reals

anchor.snippet: If the base is positive, then there exists an order homomorphism

annotations.note: `Real.ofDigits` maps digit sequences to reals and `Real.ofDigits_SurjOn` covers surjectivity onto `[0,1]`, but order-homomorphism properties on digit strings are not packaged.

annotations.label: Irrational in arbitrary base

anchor.snippet: An irrational number has a representation of infinite length that is not, from any point, an indefinitely repeating sequence of finite length

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Irrational` is defined arithmetically (not in ℚ), not via non-eventually-periodic base-b digits.
